configureCompression(ProducerConfig config) {\n543:         CompressionType type = CompressionType.forName(config.getString(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG));\n544:         switch (type) {\n545:             case GZIP: {\n546:                 return Compression.gzip()\n547:                         .level(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG))\n548:                         .build();\n549:             }\n550:             case LZ4: {\n551:                 return Compression.lz4()\n552:                         .level(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG))\n553:                         .build();\n554:             }\n555:             case ZSTD: {\n556:                 return Compression.zstd()\n557:                         .level(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG))\n558:                         .build();\n559:             }\n560:             default:\n561:                 return Compression.of(type).build();\n562:         }\n563:     }\n564: \n565:     private static int lingerMs(ProducerConfig config) {\n566:         return (int) Math.min(config.getLong(ProducerConfig.LINGER_MS_CONFIG), Integer.MAX_VALUE);\n567:     }\n568: \n569:     private static int configureDeliveryTimeout(ProducerConfig config, Logger log) {\n570:         int deliveryTimeoutMs = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n571:         int lingerMs = lingerMs(config);\n572:         int requestTimeoutMs = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n573:         int lingerAndRequestTimeoutMs = (int) Math.min((long) lingerMs + requestTimeoutMs, Integer.MAX_VALUE);\n574: \n575:         if (deliveryTimeoutMs < lingerAndRequestTimeoutMs) {\n576:             if (config.originals().containsKey(ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)) {\n577:                 // throw an exception if the user explicitly set an inconsistent value\n578:                 throw new ConfigException(ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG\n579:                     + \" should be equal to or larger than \" + ProducerConfig.LINGER_MS_CONFIG\n580:                     + \" + \" + ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n581:             } else {\n582:                 // override deliveryTimeoutMs default value to lingerMs + requestTimeoutMs for backward compatibility\n583:                 deliveryTimeoutMs = lingerAndRequestTimeoutMs;\n584:                 log.warn(\"{} should be equal to or larger than {} + {}. Setting it to {}.\",\n585:                     ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, ProducerConfig.LINGER_MS_CONFIG,\n586:                     ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, deliveryTimeoutMs);\n587:             }\n588:         }\n589:         return deliveryTimeoutMs;\n590:     }\n591: \n592:     private TransactionManager configureTransactionState(ProducerConfig config,\n593:                                                          LogContext logContext) {\n594:         TransactionManager transactionManager = null;\n595: \n596:         if (config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.ENABLE_IDEMPOTENCE_CONFIG)) {\n597:             final String transactionalId = config.getString(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CONFIG);\n598:             final boolean enable2PC = config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TWO_PHASE_COMMIT_ENABLE_CONFIG);\n599:             final int transactionTimeoutMs = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_CONFIG);\n600:             final long retryBackoffMs = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG);\n601:             \n602:             transactionManager = new TransactionManager(\n603:                 logContext,\n604:                 transactionalId,\n605:                 transactionTimeoutMs,\n606:                 retryBackoffMs,\n607:                 apiVersions,\n608:                 enable2PC\n609:             );\n610: \n611:             if (transactionManager.isTransactional())\n612:                 log.info(\"Instantiated a transactional producer.\");\n613:             else\n614:                 log.info(\"Instantiated an idempotent producer.\");\n615:         } else {\n616:             // ignore unretrieved configurations related to producer transaction\n617:             config.ignore(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_CONFIG);\n618:         }\n619:         return transactionManager;\n620:     }\n621: \n622:     /**\n623:      * Needs to be called before any other methods when the {@code transactional.id} is set in the configuration.\n624:      * This method does the following:\n625:      * <ol>\n626:      * <li>Ensures any transactions initiated by previous instances of the producer with the same\n627:      *      {@code transactional.id} are completed. If the previous instance had failed with a transaction in\n628:      *      progress, it will be aborted. If the last transaction had begun completion,\n629:      *      but not yet finished, this method awaits its completion.</li>\n630:      * <li>Gets the internal producer id and epoch, used in all future transactional\n631:      *      messages issued by the producer.</li>\n632:      * </ol>\n633:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transactional state cannot\n634:      * be initialized before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}. Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException}\n635:      * if interrupted. It is safe to retry in either case, but once the transactional state has been successfully\n636:      * initialized, this method should no longer be used.\n637:      *\n638:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no {@code transactional.id} has been configured\n639:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n640:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n641:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException error indicating that the configured\n642:      *         transactional.id is not authorized, or the idempotent producer id is unavailable. See the exception for\n643:      *         more details.  User may retry this function call after fixing the permission.\n644:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n645:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for initialize the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n646:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n647:      */\n648:     public void initTransactions() {\n649:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n650:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n651:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\n652:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.initializeTransactions(false);\n653:         sender.wakeup();\n654:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n655:         producerMetrics.recordInit(time.nanoseconds() - now);\n656:         transactionManager.maybeUpdateTransactionV2Enabled(true);\n657:     }\n658: \n659:     /**\n660:      * Should be called before the start of each new transaction. Note that prior to the first invocation\n661:      * of this method, you must invoke {@link #initTransactions()} exactly one time.\n662:      *\n663:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no {@code transactional.id} has been configured or if {@link #initTransactions()}\n664:      *         has not yet been invoked\n665:      * @throws ProducerFencedException if another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n666:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n667:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n668:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n669:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n670:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n671:      *         {@code transactional.id} is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n672:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n673:      */\n674:     public void beginTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n675:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n676:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n677:         throwIfInPreparedState();\n678:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\n679:         transactionManager.beginTransaction();\n680:         producerMetrics.recordBeginTxn(time.nanoseconds() - now);\n681:     }\n682: \n683:     /**\n684:      * Sends a list of specified offsets to the consumer group coordinator, and also marks\n685:      * those offsets as part of the current transaction. These offsets will be considered\n686:      * committed only if the transaction is committed successfully. The committed offset should\n687:      * be the next message your application will consume, i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()}\n688:      * (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}). You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata,\n689:      * which can be obtained from {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\n690:      * <p>\n691:      * This method should be used when you need to batch consumed and produced messages\n692:      * together, typically in a consume-transform-produce pattern. Thus, the specified\n693:      * {@code groupMetadata} should be extracted from the used {@link KafkaConsumer consumer} via\n694:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()} to leverage consumer group metadata. This will provide\n695:      * stronger fencing than just supplying the {@code consumerGroupId} and passing in {@code new ConsumerGroupMetadata(consumerGroupId)},\n696:      * however note that the full set of consumer group metadata returned by {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()}\n697:      * requires the brokers to be on version 2.5 or newer to understand.\n698:      *\n699:      * <p>\n700:      * This method is a blocking call that waits until the request has been received and acknowledged by the consumer group\n701:      * coordinator; but the offsets are not considered as committed until the transaction itself is successfully committed later (via\n702:      * the {@link #commitTransaction()} call).\n703:      *\n704:      * <p>\n705:      * Note, that the consumer should have {@code enable.auto.commit=false} and should\n706:      * also not commit offsets manually (via {@link KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Map) sync} or\n707:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#commitAsync(Map, OffsetCommitCallback) async} commits).\n708:      * This method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the producer cannot send offsets before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}.\n709:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n710:      *\n711:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started.\n712:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n713:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n714:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0) or\n715:      *         the broker doesn't support the latest version of transactional API with all consumer group metadata\n716:      *         (i.e. if its version is lower than 2.5.0).\n717:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException fatal error indicating the message\n718:      *         format used for the offsets topic on the broker does not support transactions\n719:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n720:      *         transactional.id is not authorized, or the consumer group id is not authorized.\n721:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried\n722:      *         (e.g. if the consumer has been kicked out of the group). Users should handle this by aborting the transaction.\n723:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this producer instance gets fenced by broker due to a\n724:      *                                                                  mis-configured consumer instance id within group metadata.\n725:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n726:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n727:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\n728:      *         other unexpected error\n729:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for sending the offsets has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n730:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n731:      */\n732:     public void sendOffsetsToTransaction(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\n733:                                          ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata) throws ProducerFencedException {\n734:         throwIfInvalidGroupMetadata(groupMetadata);\n735:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n736:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n737: \n738:         if (!offsets.isEmpty()) {\n739:             long start = time.nanoseconds();\n740:             TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.sendOffsetsToTransaction(offsets, groupMetadata);\n741:             sender.wakeup();\n742:             result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n743:             producerMetrics.recordSendOffsets(time.nanoseconds() - start);\n744:         }\n745:     }\n746: \n747:     /**\n748:      * Commits the ongoing transaction. This method will flush any unsent records before actually committing the transaction.\n749:      * <p>\n750:      * Further, if any of the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls which were part of the transaction hit irrecoverable\n751:      * errors, this method will throw the last received exception immediately and the transaction will not be committed.\n752:      * So all {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls in a transaction must succeed in order for this method to succeed.\n753:      * <p>\n754:      * If the transaction is committed successfully and this method returns without throwing an exception, it is guaranteed\n755:      * that all {@link Callback callbacks} for records in the transaction will have been invoked and completed.\n756:      * Note that exceptions thrown by callbacks are ignored; the producer proceeds to commit the transaction in any case.\n757:      * <p>\n758:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be committed before expiration\n759:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\n760:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\n761:      * to decide how to handle timeouts.\n762:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n763:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as abortTransaction)\n764:      * since the commit may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\n765:      *\n766:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\n767:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n768:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n769:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n770:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n771:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n772:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n773:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n774:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\n775:      *         other unexpected error\n776:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for committing the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n777:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n778:      */\n779:     public void commitTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n780:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n781:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n782:         long commitStart = time.nanoseconds();\n783:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginCommit();\n784:         sender.wakeup();\n785:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n786:         producerMetrics.recordCommitTxn(time.nanoseconds() - commitStart);\n787:     }\n788: \n789:     /**\n790:      * Aborts the ongoing transaction. Any unflushed produce messages will be aborted when this call is made.\n791:      * This call will throw an exception immediately if any prior {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls failed with a\n792:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} or an instance of {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}.\n793:      * <p>\n794:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be aborted before expiration\n795:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\n796:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\n797:      * to decide how to handle timeouts. Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n798:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as {@link #commitTransaction})\n799:      * since the abort may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\n800:      *\n801:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\n802:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n803:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n804:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n805:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n806:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n807:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n808:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n809:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n810:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for aborting the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n811:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n812:      */\n813:     public void abortTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n814:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n815:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n816:         log.info(\"Aborting incomplete transaction\");\n817:         long abortStart = time.nanoseconds();\n818:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginAbort();\n819:         sender.wakeup();\n820:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n821:         producerMetrics.recordAbortTxn(time.nanoseconds() - abortStart);\n822:     }\n823: \n824:     /**\n825:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic. Equivalent to <code>send(record, null)</code>.\n826:      * See {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} for details.\n827:      */\n828:     @Override\n829:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record) {\n830:         return send(record, null);\n831:     }\n832: \n833:     /**\n834:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic and invoke the provided callback when the send has been acknowledged.\n835:      * <p>\n836:      * The send is asynchronous and this method will return immediately (except for rare cases described below)\n837:      * once the record has been stored in the buffer of records waiting to be sent.\n838:      * This allows sending many records in parallel without blocking to wait for the response after each one.\n839:      * Can block for the following cases: 1) For the first record being sent to \n840:      * the cluster by this client for the given topic. In this case it will block for up to {@code max.block.ms} milliseconds if \n841:      * Kafka cluster is unreachable; 2) Allocating a buffer if buffer pool doesn't have any free buffers.\n842:      * <p>\n843:      * The result of the send is a {@link RecordMetadata} specifying the partition the record was sent to, the offset\n844:      * it was assigned and the timestamp of the record. If the producer is configured with acks = 0, the {@link RecordMetadata}\n845:      * will have offset = -1 because the producer does not wait for the acknowledgement from the broker.\n846:      * If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME CreateTime} is used by the topic, the timestamp\n847:      * will be the user provided timestamp or the record send time if the user did not specify a timestamp for the\n848:      * record. If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#LOG_APPEND_TIME LogAppendTime} is used for the\n849:      * topic, the timestamp will be the Kafka broker local time when the message is appended.\n850:      * <p>\n851:      * Since the send call is asynchronous it returns a {@link java.util.concurrent.Future Future} for the\n852:      * {@link RecordMetadata} that will be assigned to this record. Invoking {@link java.util.concurrent.Future#get()\n853:      * get()} on this future will block until the associated request completes and then return the metadata for the record\n854:      * or throw any exception that occurred while sending the record.\n855:      * <p>\n856:      * If you want to simulate a simple blocking call you can call the <code>get()</code> method immediately:\n857:      *\n858:      * <pre>\n859:      * {@code\n860:      * byte[] key = \"key\".getBytes();\n861:      * byte[] value = \"value\".getBytes();\n862:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\"my-topic\", key, value)\n863:      * producer.send(record).get();\n864:      * }</pre>\n865:      * <p>\n866:      * Fully non-blocking usage can make use of the {@link Callback} parameter to provide a callback that\n867:      * will be invoked when the request is complete.\n868:      *\n869:      * <pre>\n870:      * {@code\n871:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\"the-topic\", key, value);\n872:      * producer.send(myRecord,\n873:      *               new Callback() {\n874:      *                   public void onCompletion(RecordMetadata metadata, Exception e) {\n875:      *                       if(e != null) {\n876:      *                          e.printStackTrace();\n877:      *                       } else {\n878:      *                          System.out.println(\"The offset of the record we just sent is: \" + metadata.offset());\n879:      *                       }\n880:      *                   }\n881:      *               });\n882:      * }\n883:      * </pre>\n884:      *\n885:      * Callbacks for records being sent to the same partition are guaranteed to execute in order. That is, in the\n886:      * following example <code>callback1</code> is guaranteed to execute before <code>callback2</code>:\n887:      *\n888:      * <pre>\n889:      * {@code\n890:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key1, value1), callback1);\n891:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key2, value2), callback2);\n892:      * }\n893:      * </pre>\n894:      * <p>\n895:      * When used as part of a transaction, it is not necessary to define a callback or check the result of the future\n896:      * in order to detect errors from <code>send</code>. If any of the send calls failed with an irrecoverable error,\n897:      * the final {@link #commitTransaction()} call will fail and throw the exception from the last failed send. When\n898:      * this happens, your application should call {@link #abortTransaction()} to reset the state and continue to send\n899:      * data.\n900:      * </p>\n901:      * <p>\n902:      * Some transactional send errors cannot be resolved with a call to {@link #abortTransaction()}.  In particular,\n903:      * if a transactional send finishes with a {@link ProducerFencedException}, a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.OutOfOrderSequenceException},\n904:      * a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException}, or an\n905:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}, then the only option left is to call {@link #close()}.\n906:      * Fatal errors cause the producer to enter a defunct state in which future API calls will continue to raise\n907:      * the same underlying error wrapped in a new {@link KafkaException}.\n908:      * </p>\n909:      * <p>\n910:      * It is a similar picture when idempotence is enabled, but no <code>transactional.id</code> has been configured.\n911:      * In this case, {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException} and\n912:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException} are considered fatal errors. However,\n913:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} does not need to be handled. Additionally, it is possible to continue\n914:      * sending after receiving an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.OutOfOrderSequenceException}, but doing so\n915:      * can result in out of order delivery of pending messages. To ensure proper ordering, you should close the\n916:      * producer and create a new instance.\n917:      * </p>\n918:      * <p>\n919:      * If the message format of the destination topic is not upgraded to 0.11.0.0, idempotent and transactional\n920:      * produce requests will fail with an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException}\n921:      * error. If this is encountered during a transaction, it is possible to abort and continue. But note that future\n922:      * sends to the same topic will continue receiving the same exception until the topic is upgraded.\n923:      * </p>\n924:      * <p>\n925:      * Note that callbacks will generally execute in the I/O thread of the producer and so should be reasonably fast or\n926:      * they will delay the sending of messages from other threads. If you want to execute blocking or computationally\n927:      * expensive callbacks it is recommended to use your own {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} in the callback body\n928:      * to parallelize processing.\n929:      *\n930:      * @param record The record to send\n931:      * @param callback A user-supplied callback to execute when the record has been acknowledged by the server (null\n932:      *        indicates no callback)\n933:      *\n934:      * @throws IllegalStateException if a transactional.id has been configured and no transaction has been started, or\n935:      *                               when send is invoked after producer has been closed.\n936:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\n937:      * @throws SerializationException If the key or value are not valid objects given the configured serializers\n938:      * @throws KafkaException If a Kafka related error occurs that does not belong to the public API exceptions.\n939:      */\n940:     @Override\n941:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, Callback callback) {\n942:         // intercept the record, which can be potentially modified; this method does not throw exceptions\n943:         ProducerRecord<K, V> interceptedRecord = this.interceptors.onSend(record);\n944:         return doSend(interceptedRecord, callback);\n945:     }\n946: \n947:     // Verify that this producer instance has not been closed. This method throws IllegalStateException if the producer\n948:     // has already been closed.\n949:     private void throwIfProducerClosed() {\n950:         if (sender == null || !sender.isRunning())\n951:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Cannot perform operation after producer has been closed\");\n952:     }\n953: \n954:     /**\n955:      * Throws an exception if the transaction is in a prepared state.\n956:      * In a two-phase commit (2PC) flow, once a transaction enters the prepared state,\n957:      * only commit, abort, or complete operations are allowed.\n958:      *\n959:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any other operation is attempted in the prepared state.\n960:      */\n961:     private void throwIfInPreparedState() {\n962:         if (transactionManager != null &&\n963:             transactionManager.isTransactional() &&\n964:             transactionManager.isPrepared()\n965:         ) {\n966:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Cannot perform operation while the transaction is in a prepared state. \" +\n967:                 \"Only commitTransaction(), abortTransaction(), or completeTransaction() are permitted.\");\n968:         }\n969:     }\n970: \n971:     /**\n972:      * Implementation of asynchronously send a record to a topic.\n973:      */\n974:     private Future<RecordMetadata> doSend(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, Callback callback) {\n975:         // Append callback takes care of the following:\n976:         //  - call interceptors and user callback on completion\n977:         //  - remember partition that is calculated in RecordAccumulator.append\n978:         AppendCallbacks appendCallbacks = new AppendCallbacks(callback, this.interceptors, record);\n979: \n980:         try {\n981:             throwIfProducerClosed();\n982:             throwIfInPreparedState();\n983: \n984:             // first make sure the metadata for the topic is available\n985:             long nowMs = time.milliseconds();\n986:             ClusterAndWaitTime clusterAndWaitTime;\n987:             try {\n988:                 clusterAndWaitTime = waitOnMetadata(record.topic(), record.partition(), nowMs, maxBlockTimeMs);\n989:             } catch (KafkaException e) {\n990:                 if (metadata.isClosed())\n991:                     throw new KafkaException(\"Producer closed while send in progress\", e);\n992:                 throw e;\n993:             }\n994:             nowMs += clusterAndWaitTime.waitedOnMetadataMs;\n995:             long remainingWaitMs = Math.max(0, maxBlockTimeMs - clusterAndWaitTime.waitedOnMetadataMs);\n996:             Cluster cluster = clusterAndWaitTime.cluster;\n997:             byte[] serializedKey;\n998:             try {\n999:                 serializedKey = keySerializerPlugin.get().serialize(record.topic(), record.headers(), record.key());\n1000:             } catch (ClassCastException cce) {\n1001:                 throw new SerializationException(\"Can't convert key of class \" + record.key().getClass().getName() +\n1002:                         \" to class \" + producerConfig.getClass(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG).getName() +\n1003:                         \" specified in key.serializer\", cce);\n1004:             }\n1005:             byte[] serializedValue;\n1006:             try {\n1007:                 serializedValue = valueSerializerPlugin.get().serialize(record.topic(), record.headers(), record.value());\n1008:             } catch (ClassCastException cce) {\n1009:                 throw new SerializationException(\"Can't convert value of class \" + record.value().getClass().getName() +\n1010:                         \" to class \" + producerConfig.getClass(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG).getName() +\n1011:                         \" specified in value.serializer\", cce);\n1012:             }\n1013: \n1014:             // Try to calculate partition, but note that after this call it can be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION,\n1015:             // which means that the RecordAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n1016:             // take into account broker load, the amount of data produced to each partition, etc.).\n1017:             int partition = partition(record, serializedKey, serializedValue, cluster);\n1018: \n1019:             setReadOnly(record.headers());\n1020:             Header[] headers = record.headers().toArray();\n1021: \n1022:             int serializedSize = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytesUpperBound(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n1023:                     compression.type(), serializedKey, serializedValue, headers);\n1024:             ensureValidRecordSize(serializedSize);\n1025:             long timestamp = record.timestamp() == null ? nowMs : record.timestamp();\n1026: \n1027:             // Append the record to the accumulator.  Note, that the actual partition may be\n1028:             // calculated there and can be accessed via appendCallbacks.topicPartition.\n1029:             RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n1030:                     serializedValue, headers, appendCallbacks, remainingWaitMs, nowMs, cluster);\n1031:             assert appendCallbacks.getPartition() != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\n1032: \n1033:             // Add the partition to the transaction (if in progress) after it has been successfully\n1034:             // appended to the accumulator. We cannot do it before because the partition may be\n1035:             // unknown. Note that the `Sender` will refuse to dequeue\n1036:             // batches from the accumulator until they have been added to the transaction.\n1037:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n1038:                 transactionManager.maybeAddPartition(appendCallbacks.topicPartition());\n1039:             }\n1040: \n1041:             if (result.batchIsFull || result.newBatchCreated) {\n1042:                 log.trace(\"Waking up the sender since topic {} partition {} is either full or getting a new batch\", record.topic(), appendCallbacks.getPartition());\n1043:                 this.sender.wakeup();\n1044:             }\n1045:             return result.future;\n1046:             // handling exceptions and record the errors;\n1047:             // for API exceptions return them in the future,\n1048:             // for other exceptions throw directly\n1049:         } catch (ApiException e) {\n1050:             log.debug(\"Exception occurred during message send:\", e);\n1051:             if (callback != null) {\n1052:                 TopicPartition tp = appendCallbacks.topicPartition();\n1053:                 RecordMetadata nullMetadata = new RecordMetadata(tp, -1, -1, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, -1, -1);\n1054:                 callback.onCompletion(nullMetadata, e);\n1055:             }\n1056:             this.errors.record();\n1057:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1058:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n1059:                 transactionManager.maybeTransitionToErrorState(e);\n1060:             }\n1061:             return new FutureFailure(e);\n1062:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1063:             this.errors.record();\n1064:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1065:             throw new InterruptException(e);\n1066:         } catch (KafkaException e) {\n1067:             this.errors.record();\n1068:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1069:             throw e;\n1070:         } catch (Exception e) {\n1071:             // we notify interceptor about all exceptions, since onSend is called before anything else in this method\n1072:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1073:             throw e;\n1074:         }\n1075:     }\n1076: \n1077:     private void setReadOnly(Headers headers) {\n1078:         if (headers instanceof RecordHeaders) {\n1079:             ((RecordHeaders) headers).setReadOnly();\n1080:         }\n1081:     }\n1082: \n1083:     /**\n1084:      * Wait for cluster metadata including partitions for the given topic to be available.\n1085:      * @param topic The topic we want metadata for\n1086:      * @param partition A specific partition expected to exist in metadata, or null if there's no preference\n1087:      * @param nowMs The current time in ms\n1088:      * @param maxWaitMs The maximum time in ms for waiting on the metadata\n1089:      * @return The cluster containing topic metadata and the amount of time we waited in ms\n1090:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\n1091:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\n1092:      */\n1093:     private ClusterAndWaitTime waitOnMetadata(String topic, Integer partition, long nowMs, long maxWaitMs) throws InterruptedException {\n1094:         Cluster cluster = metadata.fetch();\n1095: \n1096:         if (cluster.invalidTopics().contains(topic))\n1097:             throw new InvalidTopicException(topic);\n1098: \n1099:         // add topic to metadata topic list if it is not there already and reset expiry\n1100:         metadata.add(topic, nowMs);\n1101: \n1102:         Integer partitionsCount = cluster.partitionCountForTopic(topic);\n1103:         // Return cached metadata if we have it, and if the record's partition is either undefined\n1104:         // or within the known partition range\n1105:         if (partitionsCount != null && (partition == null || partition < partitionsCount))\n1106:             return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, 0);\n1107: \n1108:         long remainingWaitMs = maxWaitMs;\n1109:         long elapsed = 0;\n1110:         // Issue metadata requests until we have metadata for the topic and the requested partition,\n1111:         // or until maxWaitTimeMs is exceeded. This is necessary in case the metadata\n1112:         // is stale and the number of partitions for this topic has increased in the meantime.\n1113:         long nowNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n1114:         do {\n1115:             if (partition != null) {\n1116:                 log.trace(\"Requesting metadata update for partition {} of topic {}.\", partition, topic);\n1117:             } else {\n1118:                 log.trace(\"Requesting metadata update for topic {}.\", topic);\n1119:             }\n1120:             metadata.add(topic, nowMs + elapsed);\n1121:             int version = metadata.requestUpdateForTopic(topic);\n1122:             sender.wakeup();\n1123:             try {\n1124:                 metadata.awaitUpdate(version, remainingWaitMs);\n1125:             } catch (TimeoutException ex) {\n1126:                 // Rethrow with original maxWaitMs to prevent logging exception with remainingWaitMs\n1127:                 final String errorMessage = getErrorMessage(partitionsCount, topic, partition, maxWaitMs);\n1128:                 if (metadata.getError(topic) != null) {\n1129:                     throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage, metadata.getError(topic).exception());\n1130:                 }\n1131:                 throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage);\n1132:             }\n1133:             cluster = metadata.fetch();\n1134:             elapsed = time.milliseconds() - nowMs;\n1135:             if (elapsed >= maxWaitMs) {\n1136:                 final String errorMessage = getErrorMessage(partitionsCount, topic, partition, maxWaitMs);\n1137:                 if (metadata.getError(topic) != null && metadata.getError(topic).exception() instanceof RetriableException) {\n1138:                     throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage, metadata.getError(topic).exception());\n1139:                 }\n1140:                 throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage);\n1141:             }\n1142:             metadata.maybeThrowExceptionForTopic(topic);\n1143:             remainingWaitMs = maxWaitMs - elapsed;\n1144:             partitionsCount = cluster.partitionCountForTopic(topic);\n1145:         } while (partitionsCount == null || (partition != null && partition >= partitionsCount));\n1146: \n1147:         producerMetrics.recordMetadataWait(time.nanoseconds() - nowNanos);\n1148: \n1149:         return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, elapsed);\n1150:     }\n1151: \n1152:     private String getErrorMessage(Integer partitionsCount, String topic, Integer partition, long maxWaitMs) {\n1153:         return partitionsCount == null ?\n1154:             String.format(\"Topic %s not present in metadata after %d ms.\",\n1155:                 topic, maxWaitMs) :\n1156:             String.format(\"Partition %d of topic %s with partition count %d is not present in metadata after %d ms.\",\n1157:                 partition, topic, partitionsCount, maxWaitMs);\n1158:     }\n1159:     /**\n1160:      * Validate that the record size isn't too large\n1161:      */\n1162:     private void ensureValidRecordSize(int size) {\n1163:         if (size > maxRequestSize)\n1164:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\"The message is \" + size +\n1165:                     \" bytes when serialized which is larger than \" + maxRequestSize + \", which is the value of the \" +\n1166:                     ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG + \" configuration.\");\n1167:         if (size > totalMemorySize)\n1168:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\"The message is \" + size +\n1169:                     \" bytes when serialized which is larger than the total memory buffer you have configured with the \" +\n1170:                     ProducerConfig.BUFFER_MEMORY_CONFIG +\n1171:                     \" configuration.\");\n1172:     }\n1173: \n1174:     /**\n1175:      * Invoking this method makes all buffered records immediately available to send (even if <code>linger.ms</code> is\n1176:      * greater than 0) and blocks on the completion of the requests associated with these records. The post-condition\n1177:      * of <code>flush()</code> is that any previously sent record will have completed (e.g. <code>Future.isDone() == true</code>\n1178:      * and callbacks passed to {@link #send(ProducerRecord,Callback)} have been called).\n1179:      * A request is considered completed when it is successfully acknowledged\n1180:      * according to the <code>acks</code> configuration you have specified or else it results in an error.\n1181:      * <p>\n1182:      * Other threads can continue sending records while one thread is blocked waiting for a flush call to complete,\n1183:      * however no guarantee is made about the completion of records sent after the flush call begins.\n1184:      * <p>\n1185:      * This method can be useful when consuming from some input system and producing into Kafka. The <code>flush()</code> call\n1186:      * gives a convenient way to ensure all previously sent messages have actually completed.\n1187:      * <p>\n1188:      * This example shows how to consume from one Kafka topic and produce to another Kafka topic:\n1189:      * <pre>\n1190:      * {@code\n1191:      * for(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record: consumer.poll(100))\n1192:      *     producer.send(new ProducerRecord(\"my-topic\", record.key(), record.value());\n1193:      * producer.flush();\n1194:      * consumer.commitSync();\n1195:      * }\n1196:      * </pre>\n1197:      *\n1198:      * Note that the above example may drop records if the produce request fails. If we want to ensure that this does not occur\n1199:      * we need to set <code>retries=&lt;large_number&gt;</code> in our config.\n1200:      * </p>\n1201:      * <p>\n1202:      * Applications don't need to call this method for transactional producers, since the {@link #commitTransaction()} will\n1203:      * flush all buffered records before performing the commit. This ensures that all the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\n1204:      * calls made since the previous {@link #beginTransaction()} are completed before the commit.\n1205:      * </p>\n1206:      * <p>\n1207:      * <b>Important:</b> This method must not be called from within the callback provided to\n1208:      * {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)}. Invoking <code>flush()</code> in this context will result in a\n1209:      * {@link KafkaException} being thrown, as it will cause a deadlock.\n1210:      * </p>\n1211:      *\n1212:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\n1213:      * @throws KafkaException If the method is invoked inside a {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} callback\n1214:      */\n1215:     @Override\n1216:     public void flush() {\n1217:         if (Thread.currentThread() == this.ioThread) {\n1218:             log.error(\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\");\n1219:             throw new KafkaException(\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\");\n1220:         }\n1221: \n1222:         log.trace(\"Flushing accumulated records in producer.\");\n1223: \n1224:         long start = time.nanoseconds();\n1225:         this.accumulator.beginFlush();\n1226:         this.sender.wakeup();\n1227:         try {\n1228:             this.accumulator.awaitFlushCompletion();\n1229:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1230:             throw new InterruptException(\"Flush interrupted.\", e);\n1231:         } finally {\n1232:             producerMetrics.recordFlush(time.nanoseconds() - start);\n1233:         }\n1234:     }\n1235: \n1236:     /**\n1237:      * Get the partition metadata for the given topic. This can be used for custom partitioning.\n1238:      * @throws AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1239:      * @throws AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See the exception for more details\n1240:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n1241:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\n1242:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\n1243:      */\n1244:     @Override\n1245:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic) {\n1246:         Objects.requireNonNull(topic, \"topic cannot be null\");\n1247:         try {\n1248:             return waitOnMetadata(topic, null, time.milliseconds(), maxBlockTimeMs).cluster.partitionsForTopic(topic);\n1249:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1250:             throw new InterruptException(e);\n1251:         }\n1252:     }\n1253: \n1254:     /**\n1255:      * Get the full set of internal metrics maintained by the producer.\n1256:      */\n1257:     @Override\n1258:     public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\n1259:         return Collections.unmodifiableMap(this.metrics.metrics());\n1260:     }\n1261: \n1262: \n1263:     /**\n1264:      * Add the provided application metric for subscription.\n1265:      * This metric will be added to this client's metrics\n1266:      * that are available for subscription and sent as\n1267:      * telemetry data to the broker.\n1268:      * The provided metric must map to an OTLP metric data point\n1269:      * type in the OpenTelemetry v1 metrics protobuf message types.\n1270:      * Specifically, the metric should be one of the following:\n1271:      * <ul>\n1272:      *  <li>\n1273:      *     `Sum`: Monotonic total count meter (Counter). Suitable for metrics like total number of X, e.g., total bytes sent.\n1274:      *  </li>\n1275:      *  <li>\n1276:      *     `Gauge`: Non-monotonic current value meter (UpDownCounter). Suitable for metrics like current value of Y, e.g., current queue count.\n1277:      *  </li>\n1278:      * </ul>\n1279:      * Metrics not matching these types are silently ignored.\n1280:      * Executing this method for a previously registered metric is a benign operation and results in updating that metrics entry.\n1281:      *\n1282:      * @param metric The application metric to register\n1283:      */\n1284:     @Override\n1285:     public void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1286:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\n1287:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricChange(metric));\n1288:         }  else {\n1289:             log.debug(\"Skipping registration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be overwritten.\", metric.metricName());\n1290:         }\n1291:     }\n1292: \n1293:     /**\n1294:      * Remove the provided application metric for subscription.\n1295:      * This metric is removed from this client's metrics\n1296:      * and will not be available for subscription any longer.\n1297:      * Executing this method with a metric that has not been registered is a\n1298:      * benign operation and does not result in any action taken (no-op).\n1299:      *\n1300:      * @param metric The application metric to remove\n1301:      */\n1302:     @Override\n1303:     public void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1304:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\n1305:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricRemoval(metric));\n1306:         } else {\n1307:             log.debug(\"Skipping unregistration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be removed.\", metric.metricName());\n1308:         }\n1309:     }\n1310: \n1311:     /**\n1312:      * Determines the client's unique client instance ID used for telemetry. This ID is unique to\n1313:      * this specific client instance and will not change after it is initially generated.\n1314:      * The ID is useful for correlating client operations with telemetry sent to the broker and\n1315:      * to its eventual monitoring destinations.\n1316:      * <p>\n1317:      * If telemetry is enabled, this will first require a connection to the cluster to generate\n1318:      * the unique client instance ID. This method waits up to {@code timeout} for the producer\n1319:      * client to complete the request.\n1320:      * <p>\n1321:      * Client telemetry is controlled by the {@link ProducerConfig#ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG}\n1322:      * configuration option.\n1323:      *\n1324:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer client to determine its client instance ID.\n1325:      *                The value must be non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not\n1326:      *                wait for the initial request to complete if it hasn't already.\n1327:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1328:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to determine the client\n1329:      *                        instance ID, though this error does not necessarily imply the\n1330:      *                        producer client is otherwise unusable.\n1331:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\n1332:      * @throws IllegalStateException If telemetry is not enabled ie, config `{@code enable.metrics.push}`\n1333:      *                               is set to `{@code false}`.\n1334:      * @return The client's assigned instance id used for metrics collection.\n1335:      */\n1336:     @Override\n1337:     public Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout) {\n1338:         if (clientTelemetryReporter.isEmpty()) {\n1339:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Telemetry is not enabled. Set config `\" + ProducerConfig.ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG + \"` to `true`.\");\n1340:         }\n1341: \n1342:         return ClientTelemetryUtils.fetchClientInstanceId(clientTelemetryReporter.get(), timeout);\n1343:     }\n1344: \n1345:     /**\n1346:      * Close this producer. This method blocks until all previously sent requests complete.\n1347:      * This method is equivalent to <code>close(Long.MAX_VALUE, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)</code>.\n1348:      * <p>\n1349:      * <strong>If close() is called from {@link Callback}, a warning message will be logged and close(0, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n1350:      * will be called instead. We do this because the sender thread would otherwise try to join itself and\n1351:      * block forever.</strong>\n1352:      * <p>\n1353:      *\n1354:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1355:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\n1356:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\n1357:      */\n1358:     @Override\n1359:     public void close() {\n1360:         close(Duration.ofMillis(Long.MAX_VALUE));\n1361:     }\n1362: \n1363:     /**\n1364:      * This method waits up to <code>timeout</code> for the producer to complete the sending of all incomplete requests.\n1365:      * <p>\n1366:      * If the producer is unable to complete all requests before the timeout expires, this method will fail\n1367:      * any unsent and unacknowledged records immediately. It will also abort the ongoing transaction if it's not\n1368:      * already completing.\n1369:      * <p>\n1370:      * If invoked from within a {@link Callback} this method will not block and will be equivalent to\n1371:      * <code>close(Duration.ofMillis(0))</code>. This is done since no further sending will happen while\n1372:      * blocking the I/O thread of the producer.\n1373:      *\n1374:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer to complete any pending requests. The value should be\n1375:      *                non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not wait for pending send requests to complete.\n1376:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1377:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\n1378:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\n1379:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the <code>timeout</code> is negative.\n1380:      *\n1381:      */\n1382:     @Override\n1383:     public void close(Duration timeout) {\n1384:         close(timeout, false);\n1385:     }\n1386: \n1387:     private void close(Duration timeout, boolean swallowException) {\n1388:         long timeoutMs = timeout.toMillis();\n1389:         if (timeoutMs < 0)\n1390:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The timeout cannot be negative.\");\n1391:         log.info(\"Closing the Kafka producer with timeoutMillis = {} ms.\", timeoutMs);\n1392: \n1393:         // this will keep track of the first encountered exception\n1394:         AtomicReference<Throwable> firstException = new AtomicReference<>();\n1395:         boolean invokedFromCallback = Thread.currentThread() == this.ioThread;\n1396:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {\n1397:             if (invokedFromCallback) {\n1398:                 log.warn(\"Overriding close timeout {} ms to 0 ms in order to prevent useless blocking due to self-join. \" +\n1399:                         \"This means you have incorrectly invoked close with a non-zero timeout from the producer call-back.\",\n1400:                         timeoutMs);\n1401:             } else {\n1402:                 // Try to close gracefully.\n1403:                 final Timer closeTimer = time.timer(timeout);\n1404:                 clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(ClientTelemetryReporter::initiateClose);\n1405:                 closeTimer.update();\n1406: \n1407:                 if (this.sender != null) {\n1408:                     this.sender.initiateClose();\n1409:                     closeTimer.update();\n1410:                 }\n1411:                 if (this.ioThread != null) {\n1412:                     try {\n1413:                         this.ioThread.join(closeTimer.remainingMs());\n1414:                     } catch (InterruptedException t) {\n1415:                         firstException.compareAndSet(null, new InterruptException(t));\n1416:                         log.error(\"Interrupted while joining ioThread\", t);\n1417:                     } finally {\n1418:                         closeTimer.update();\n1419:                     }\n1420:                 }\n1421:             }\n1422:         }\n1423: \n1424:         if (this.sender != null && this.ioThread != null && this.ioThread.isAlive()) {\n1425:             log.info(\"Proceeding to force close the producer since pending requests could not be completed \" +\n1426:                     \"within timeout {} ms.\", timeoutMs);\n1427:             this.sender.forceClose();\n1428:             // Only join the sender thread when not calling from callback.\n1429:             if (!invokedFromCallback) {\n1430:                 try {\n1431:                     this.ioThread.join();\n1432:                 } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1433:                     firstException.compareAndSet(null, new InterruptException(e));\n1434:                 }\n1435:             }\n1436:         }\n1437: \n1438:         Utils.closeQuietly(interceptors, \"producer interceptors\", firstException);\n1439:         Utils.closeQuietly(producerMetrics, \"producer metrics wrapper\", firstException);\n1440:         Utils.closeQuietly(metrics, \"producer metrics\", firstException);\n1441:         Utils.closeQuietly(keySerializerPlugin, \"producer keySerializer\", firstException);\n1442:         Utils.closeQuietly(valueSerializerPlugin, \"producer valueSerializer\", firstException);\n1443:         Utils.closeQuietly(partitionerPlugin, \"producer partitioner\", firstException);\n1444:         clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> Utils.closeQuietly(reporter, \"producer telemetry reporter\", firstException));\n1445:         AppInfoParser.unregisterAppInfo(JMX_PREFIX, clientId, metrics);\n1446:         Throwable exception = firstException.get();\n1447:         if (exception != null && !swallowException) {\n1448:             if (exception instanceof InterruptException) {\n1449:                 throw (InterruptException) exception;\n1450:             }\n1451:             throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to close kafka producer\", exception);\n1452:         }\n1453:         log.debug(\"Kafka producer has been closed\");\n1454:     }\n1455: \n1456:     /**\n1457:      * computes partition for given record.\n1458:      * if the record has partition returns the value otherwise\n1459:      * if custom partitioner is specified, call it to compute partition\n1460:      * otherwise try to calculate partition based on key.\n1461:      * If there is no key or key should be ignored return\n1462:      * RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION to indicate any partition\n1463:      * can be used (the partition is then calculated by built-in\n1464:      * partitioning logic).\n1465:      */\n1466:     private int partition(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, byte[] serializedKey, byte[] serializedValue, Cluster cluster) {\n1467:         if (record.partition() != null)\n1468:             return record.partition();\n1469: \n1470:         if (partitionerPlugin.get() != null) {\n1471:             int customPartition = partitionerPlugin.get().partition(\n1472:                 record.topic(), record.key(), serializedKey, record.value(), serializedValue, cluster);\n1473:             if (customPartition < 0) {\n1474:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(String.format(\n1475:                     \"The partitioner generated an invalid partition number: %d. Partition number should always be non-negative.\", customPartition));\n1476:             }\n1477:             return customPartition;\n1478:         }\n1479: \n1480:         if (serializedKey != null && !partitionerIgnoreKeys) {\n1481:             // hash the keyBytes to choose a partition\n1482:             return BuiltInPartitioner.partitionForKey(serializedKey, cluster.partitionsForTopic(record.topic()).size());\n1483:         } else {\n1484:             return RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\n1485:         }\n1486:     }\n1487: \n1488:     private void throwIfInvalidGroupMetadata(ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata) {\n1489:         if (groupMetadata == null) {\n1490:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Consumer group metadata could not be null\");\n1491:         } else if (groupMetadata.generationId() > 0\n1492:             && JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID.equals(groupMetadata.memberId())) {\n1493:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Passed in group metadata \" + groupMetadata + \" has generationId > 0 but the member.id is unknown\");\n1494:         }\n1495:     }\n1496: \n1497:     private void throwIfNoTransactionManager() {\n1498:         if (transactionManager == null)\n1499:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Cannot use transactional methods without enabling transactions \" +\n1500:                     \"by setting the \" + ProducerConfig.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CONFIG + \" configuration property\");\n1501:     }\n1502: \n1503:     // Visible for testing\n1504:     String getClientId() {\n1505:         return clientId;\n1506:     }\n1507: \n1508:     private static class ClusterAndWaitTime {\n1509:         final Cluster cluster;\n1510:         final long waitedOnMetadataMs;\n1511:         ClusterAndWaitTime(Cluster cluster, long waitedOnMetadataMs) {\n1512:             this.cluster = cluster;\n1513:             this.waitedOnMetadataMs = waitedOnMetadataMs;\n1514:         }\n1515:     }\n1516: \n1517:     private static class FutureFailure implements Future<RecordMetadata> {\n1518: \n1519:         private final ExecutionException exception;\n1520: \n1521:         public FutureFailure(Exception exception) {\n1522:             this.exception = new ExecutionException(exception);\n1523:         }\n1524: \n1525:         @Override\n1526:         public boolean cancel(boolean interrupt) {\n1527:             return false;\n1528:         }\n1529: \n1530:         @Override\n1531:         public RecordMetadata get() throws ExecutionException {\n1532:             throw this.exception;\n1533:         }\n1534: \n1535:         @Override\n1536:         public RecordMetadata get(long timeout, TimeUnit unit) throws ExecutionException {\n1537:             throw this.exception;\n1538:         }\n1539: \n1540:         @Override\n1541:         public boolean isCancelled() {\n1542:             return false;\n1543:         }\n1544: \n1545:         @Override\n1546:         public boolean isDone() {\n1547:             return true;\n1548:         }\n1549: \n1550:     }\n1551: \n1552:     /**\n1553:      * Callbacks that are called by the RecordAccumulator append functions:\n1554:      *  - user callback\n1555:      *  - interceptor callbacks\n1556:      *  - partition callback\n1557:      */\n1558:     private class AppendCallbacks implements RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks {\n1559:         private final Callback userCallback;\n1560:         private final ProducerInterceptors<K, V> interceptors;\n1561:         private final String topic;\n1562:         private final Integer recordPartition;\n1563:         private final String recordLogString;\n1564:         private volatile int partition = RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\n1565:         private volatile TopicPartition topicPartition;\n1566:         private final Headers headers;\n1567: \n1568:         private AppendCallbacks(Callback userCallback, ProducerInterceptors<K, V> interceptors, ProducerRecord<K, V> record) {\n1569:             this.userCallback = userCallback;\n1570:             this.interceptors = interceptors;\n1571: }\\n356: \\n357:     private long sendProducerData(long now) {\\n358:         MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot = metadata.fetchMetadataSnapshot();\\n359:         // get the list of partitions with to send to\\n376:         Iterator<Node> iter = result.readyNodes.iterator();\\n377:         long notReadyTimeout = Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n378:         while (iter.hasNext()) {\\n379:             Node node Otherwise, the timeout will be the smaller value between next batch expiry\\n428:         // time, and the delay time for checking data availability. Note that the nodes may have data that isn't yet\\n429:         // sendable due to lingering, backing off, etc. This specifically does not include nodes with sendable data\\n430:         // that
